task_completion.description: Load the three specified FITS images into AstroImageJ.
task_completion.visual_evidence: AstroImageJ interface is open with image windows displaying the contents of 502nmos.fits, 656nmos.fits, and 673nmos.fits.

task_completion.description: Adjust the contrast of the images so nebula structures are clearly visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'B&C' (Brightness & Contrast) window is visible and being adjusted, or the nebula structures (Pillars of Creation) are clearly visible and stretched in the image windows compared to a dark raw FITS state.

task_completion.description: Open the Montage tool and configure a 3x1 layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Make Montage...' dialog box is open with 'Columns' set to 3 and 'Rows' set to 1.

task_completion.description: Set the scale factor to 0.5 and enable slice labels in the Montage tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Make Montage...' dialog box, the 'Scale factor' field is set to 0.5 and the 'Label slices' checkbox is checked.
A new image window titled 'Montage' (or similar) is visible, displaying a single row of three nebula images with text labels on each slice.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting montage as a PNG to the specified publication directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box or file manager shows the file being saved as 'eagle_montage.png' in the '~/AstroImages/publication/' (or '/home/ga/AstroImages/publication/') directory with the PNG format selected.

integrity.description: The agent used the AstroImageJ GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the AstroImageJ graphical interface (menus, dialogs, image windows) rather than using terminal commands like ImageMagick to bypass the application.

integrity.description: The montage contains actual astronomical data of the Eagle Nebula.
integrity.visual_evidence: The images in the montage visually resemble the Eagle Nebula (Pillars of Creation) and are not blank, random noise, or placeholder images.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file through the application rather than spoofing it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save operation is performed via AstroImageJ's 'Save As' dialog, rather than seeing terminal commands like 'touch eagle_montage.png' or copying a pre-existing file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a 3x1 montage saved exactly at '/home/ga/AstroImages/publication/eagle_montage.png'. Because the original FITS files are 1600x1600 pixels and the scale factor is 0.5, the final montage should have an aspect ratio of approximately 3:1 (specifically 2400x800 pixels, with a tolerance of 0.6 for padding). The file size should exceed 20 KB, confirming it contains real high-contrast nebular data.
